title: Modular Arithmetic and Public-Key Reasoning · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent team receives different assets, adversaries, data sizes, cryptoperiods, interoperability duties, availability limits, and compliance claims and transfers modular arithmetic and public-key reasoning into a new bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded modular arithmetic reasoning notebook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives.
Use Euclid and extended Euclid to determine coprimality and modular inverses and identify non-invertible cases.
Trace square-and-multiply cost and state why secret exponent code must use vetted constant-time implementations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Crypto Go Modular Congruence
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab cryptographic engineering team handles case 3512 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to normalize modular values and distinguish mathematical correctness from approved constant-time group execution; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Crypto Go Modular Congruence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compute and explain modular equivalence, reduction, addition, multiplication, exponentiation, and negative representatives. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Crypto Go Modular Congruence, keep Crypto Go Modular Congruence connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
